Paper_Metadata.Paper_Title: Highly printable, strong, and ductile ordered intermetallic alloy

Composition_Info.Note: Pre-alloyed powder composition. Thermal-stabilized CCIMA measured by SEM-EDS: Ni58.1Co19.5Si11.5Ti6.6Al4.3 (at%)

Process_Text.original: LPBF using SLM 125HL machine equipped with IPG fiber laser (max 400 W, beam diameter 64 μm); laser power 200 W; scanning speed 900 mm/s; layer thickness 0.03 mm; hatch distance 0.12 mm; layer rotation 67°; build plate 316L stainless steel heated to 200°C; argon atmosphere with oxygen <400 ppm; followed by thermal stabilization at 1000°C for 2 h with air cooling.
Process_Text.simplified: LPBF (200 W, 900 mm/s) + thermal stabilization (1000°C/2h/air cooling)

Process_Info.Equipment: SLM 125HL (IPG fiber laser, max 400 W, beam diameter 64 μm)

Microstructure_Text.original: XRD confirms ordered L12-type crystal structure. EBSD-IPF shows random grain distribution (max texture index 2.56). Grain size 17.7 μm average, fully equiaxed. SEM-EDS shows homogeneous elemental distributions. TEM-BF: dendritic G phase in as-printed eliminated after thermal treatment. SAED pattern shows highly ordered superlattice spots. APT: homogeneous element distribution within grains without elemental clustering. AC-STEM-HAADF: Ni and Co occupy face centers (A-type sublattice), Si, Ti, Al occupy vertices (B-type sublattice). Grain boundary region: 5 nm thick disordered FCC nanolayer (DINL) rich in Co and B, depleted in Ni, Si, Ti, Al. Co concentration up to 50.64±1.23 at% in DINL vs ~18.95±2.04 at% in grain interior. B segregation up to 0.85±0.22 at% in DINL vs ~0.02±0.01 at% in grain. DINL is FCC γ phase coherent with adjacent L12 grain; volume fraction ~0.08%.
Microstructure_Text.simplified: L12 ordered superlattice grains (17.7 μm avg) with 5 nm disordered FCC nanolayer (DINL) at grain boundaries enriched in Co and B; homogeneous elemental distribution

Properties_Info.Test_Condition: at 298.15 K; strain rate 1×10^-3 s^-1; perpendicular to BD; thermal-stabilized CCIMA
Properties_Info.Test_Specimen: ASTM E8, gauge length 25 mm equivalent (flat dog-bone, gauge 12.5 mm, cross-section ~3.2×3 mm^2)

Properties_Info.Test_Condition: at 298.15 K; strain rate 1×10^-3 s^-1; perpendicular to BD; thermal-stabilized CCIMA
Properties_Info.Test_Specimen: ASTM E8, gauge length 25 mm equivalent (flat dog-bone, gauge 12.5 mm, cross-section ~3.2×3 mm^2)
Properties_Info.Note: Uniform elongation; thermal-stabilized CCIMA; large uniform elongation at high strength level

Properties_Info.Test_Condition: DFT calculation for APB energy on (111) plane
DFT calculation for SISF energy on (111) plane
Calculated from f_DINL = 1-(1-t/d)^3 where t=5nm, d=17.7μm
Properties_Info.Note: Volume fraction of disordered interfacial nanolayer

Properties_Info.Test_Condition: Evolution from 5% to 35% strain; calculated by modified Williamson-Hall method
Properties_Info.Note: Rapid increase in dislocation density with deformation strain; contributes to work hardening

Properties_Info.Test_Condition: Disordered interfacial nanolayer (DINL) at grain boundaries
Properties_Info.Note: FCC disordered phase (γ) coherent with adjacent L12 grain; volume fraction ~0.08%

Properties_Info.Test_Condition: Stage II deformation; maximum WHR achieved
Properties_Info.Note: Anomalously high work hardening rate; exceeds most metallic materials including HEAs, Ni-based superalloys, and steels
